watercolors.label: Watercolors
watercolors.description: Original watercolor paintings by Imad Obegi — landscapes, botanicals, still life, and coastal scenes.

watercolors.title: Atlantic Crossing
watercolors.description: Muted blues and grays carry the weight of open water — the Atlantic in all its restless breadth, painted with loose, confident washes that capture the feeling of being far from shore.

watercolors.title: Autumn Arrival — Engine No. 3
watercolors.description: A vintage locomotive arrives through autumn color, warm ochres and rust bleeding into the surrounding foliage. History and movement, rendered in layered watercolor washes.

watercolors.title: Crimson Canna
watercolors.description: A close study of canna lilies, their petals unfolding in vivid reds and burnt oranges. The transparency of watercolor suits the luminous quality of the blooms — light seems to come from within.

watercolors.title: Emerald Isle — Beverly Waves
watercolors.description: Crashing surf along a coastline painted in layers of emerald, teal, and seafoam white. The painting holds the particular quality of Atlantic coastal light — bright and slightly cold, even in summer.

watercolors.title: Flame Lily Radiance
watercolors.description: The flame lily's reflexed petals — crimson fading to gold at the edges — are captured in confident watercolor strokes. A botanical study that doesn't sacrifice life for accuracy.

watercolors.title: Morning Light on Majolica
watercolors.description: Soft morning light falls across ornate majolica pottery, the rich glazes catching warmth and shadow. A quiet still life painted with the unhurried attention of early hours.

watercolors.title: Sentinel of the High Desert
watercolors.description: A lone saguaro stands against a wide desert sky, quiet and monumental. The painting captures the particular stillness of the high desert — the sense that something ancient is simply watching.

watercolors.title: The Crab Shack, Salter Path
watercolors.description: A weathered coastal shack in the fishing village of Salter Path, North Carolina, painted with the loose, direct strokes of plein air watercolor. Salt air and sun-bleached wood, caught before the light changed.

watercolors.title: The Spirit of Santa Monica
watercolors.description: The Santa Monica Pier at golden hour — the Ferris wheel, the Pacific, the particular glow of a California afternoon. Painted with warmth and a light hand, the way the place actually feels.

watercolors.title: The View from Home
watercolors.description: A familiar landscape seen from a place of belonging — the kind of view you stop noticing until you paint it. Rendered with quiet affection and the economy of someone who knows exactly what matters.

watercolors.title: The Vision Returns
watercolors.description: A richly layered composition where color and form do the work of language. The title suggests a return — something seen before, now seen again differently. One of the larger pieces in this collection.

watercolors.title: Wedgwood Jasperware Pitcher
watercolors.description: A careful still life study of a classic Wedgwood jasperware pitcher — its pale blue ground and white relief ornament rendered with attention to the subtle interplay of matte surface and ambient light.
